cameroon-history.name: Cameroon History
cameroon-history.description: Test your knowledge about the rich history of Cameroon from ancient kingdoms to independence.

questions.question: When did Cameroon gain independence from colonial rule?
questions.explanation: Cameroon gained independence from France on January 1, 1960, becoming a sovereign nation.

questions.question: Which European country was the first to establish colonies in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Germany was the first European country to establish colonies in Cameroon in 1884, during the Berlin Conference.

questions.question: What was the name of the German colony in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: The German colony was called Kamerun, which is where the modern name Cameroon originates.

questions.question: Which two countries controlled Cameroon after World War I?
questions.explanation: After Germany lost World War I, Cameroon was divided between France and Britain under League of Nations mandates.

questions.question: What major event occurred in Cameroon in 1982?
questions.explanation: Paul Biya became president of Cameroon in 1982, taking over from Ahmadou Ahidjo who resigned.

questions.question: Which ancient kingdom was centered in Foumban, West Cameroon?
questions.explanation: The Bamum Kingdom was centered in Foumban and was one of the most sophisticated pre-colonial kingdoms in Cameroon.

questions.question: What was the name of the famous Sultan of the Bamum Kingdom who developed a writing system?
questions.explanation: Sultan Njoya of the Bamum Kingdom developed an indigenous writing system in the late 19th century.

questions.question: Which Cameroon leader served as the first president after independence?
questions.explanation: Ahmadou Ahidjo was the first president of Cameroon after independence, serving from 1960 to 1982.

questions.question: What major conflict affected Cameroon in the 1950s and 1960s?
questions.explanation: The Kamerun War (1955-1961) was a conflict between British Southern Cameroons and Nigeria over the territory's future.

questions.question: When was the Federal Republic of Cameroon established?
questions.explanation: The Federal Republic of Cameroon was established in 1961 after the unification of French Cameroon and British Southern Cameroons.

cameroon-geography.name: Cameroon Geography
cameroon-geography.description: Discover the diverse landscapes, climate zones, and natural features of Cameroon.

questions.question: What is the highest mountain in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Mount Fako (4,040m) is the highest peak in Cameroon and is located on Mount Cameroon.

questions.question: Which ocean borders Cameroon to the west?
questions.explanation: Cameroon has a coastline along the Atlantic Ocean to the west.

questions.question: How many regions does Cameroon have?
questions.explanation: Cameroon is divided into 10 administrative regions.

questions.question: Which major river flows through Cameroon?
questions.explanation: The Benue River is one of the major rivers in Cameroon and a tributary of the Niger River.

questions.question: What type of climate is found in coastal Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Coastal Cameroon has a tropical climate with high humidity and rainfall.

questions.question: Which plateau covers much of northern Cameroon?
questions.explanation: The Adamawa Plateau covers much of northern Cameroon and is part of the larger Adamawa Highlands.

questions.question: What is the approximate area of Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Cameroon covers an area of approximately 475,000 square kilometers.

questions.question: Which mountain range is known for its volcanic activity in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: The Cameroon Line is a chain of volcanic mountains that includes Mount Cameroon, one of Africa's most active volcanoes.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest lake in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Lake Chad is the largest lake in Cameroon, though it's shared with neighboring countries.

questions.question: Which region is known as the 'Roof of Africa'?
questions.explanation: The Adamawa Region is often called the 'Roof of Africa' due to its high elevation plateau.

bamum-legacy.name: Bamum Legacy
bamum-legacy.description: Discover the ancient kingdom and innovations of the Bamum people.

questions.question: Which Bamum Sultan developed an indigenous writing system?
questions.explanation: Sultan Njoya of Bamum developed an indigenous writing system in the late 19th century.

questions.question: In which Cameroonian city was the Bamum kingdom centered?
questions.explanation: The Bamum kingdom was centered in Foumban, West-Central Cameroon.

questions.question: What material were the famous Bamum bronze sculptures made from?
questions.explanation: The Bamum were renowned for their bronze sculptures, particularly royal statues and ceremonial objects.

questions.question: What is the 'Nguon' festival commemorating?
questions.explanation: The Nguon festival commemorates the installation of Sultan Njoya and celebrates Bamum culture.

questions.question: What was the primary contribution of Sultan Njoya to Bamum culture?
questions.explanation: Sultan Njoya's greatest contribution was developing an indigenous writing system for the Bamum language.

questions.question: What is the name of the Bamum royal palace?
questions.explanation: Njoya's Palace in Foumban is the royal palace of the Bamum kingdom, now a museum.

questions.question: Which European explorer visited the Bamum kingdom in the early 20th century?
questions.explanation: Gustav Nachtigal, a German explorer, visited the Bamum kingdom in the early 1900s.

questions.question: What is the traditional Bamum art of decorating gourds called?
questions.explanation: The Bamum developed the art of decorating gourds with their indigenous script.

questions.question: What was the name of the Bamum administrative system?
questions.explanation: The Bamum kingdom was organized into a fondom system, with various administrative units.

questions.question: Which religion was adopted by the Bamum kingdom under Sultan Njoya?
questions.explanation: Sultan Njoya converted to Islam and introduced it to the Bamum kingdom.

bassa-traditions.name: Bassa Traditions
bassa-traditions.description: Learn about the customs and traditions of the Bassa people of Cameroon.

questions.question: Which region is primarily inhabited by the Bassa people?
questions.explanation: The Bassa people primarily inhabit the Littoral Region of Cameroon, particularly around Douala.

questions.question: What is the traditional Bassa staple dish?
questions.explanation: Mbongo Tchobi is the traditional Bassa dish, a spicy black stew made with fish or meat.

questions.question: What is the name of the Bassa initiation ceremony for young men?
questions.explanation: Mengue is the traditional Bassa initiation ceremony for young men, marking their transition to adulthood.

questions.question: Which Bassa festival celebrates the arrival of the first rains?
questions.explanation: The First Rain Festival celebrates the arrival of the first rains and the beginning of the farming season.

questions.question: What is the traditional Bassa fishing technique?
questions.explanation: The Bassa traditionally used plant-based poisons to stun fish in rivers for easier capture.

questions.question: What is the name of the traditional Bassa canoe?
questions.explanation: The Bassa use traditional dugout canoes for fishing and transportation along rivers.

questions.question: Which Bassa tradition involves elaborate beadwork?
questions.explanation: Bassa wedding ceremonies feature elaborate beadwork as part of traditional attire.

questions.question: What is the traditional Bassa house made of?
questions.explanation: Traditional Bassa houses are made of mud and wood with thatched roofs.

questions.question: Which Bassa dance is performed during harvest celebrations?
questions.explanation: The Bassa Folk Dance is performed during harvest celebrations and other festive occasions.

questions.question: What is the traditional Bassa musical instrument?
questions.explanation: The talking drum is a traditional Bassa musical instrument used to communicate messages.

douala-history.name: Douala History & Culture
douala-history.description: Explore the history and culture of the Douala people, Cameroon's coastal ethnic group.

questions.question: What is the traditional name for the Douala people?
questions.explanation: The Douala people are traditionally known as the Sawa people, meaning 'coastal dwellers'.

questions.question: Which European nation first established trade relations with the Douala?
questions.explanation: Germany established the first formal trade relations with the Douala in the late 19th century.

questions.question: What is the traditional Douala fishing technique using plant poisons called?
questions.explanation: Tikta is the traditional Douala fishing technique using plant-based poisons to stun fish.

questions.question: Which Douala festival celebrates the water spirits?
questions.explanation: The Ngondo festival is the most important Douala celebration, honoring the water spirits.

questions.question: What is the traditional Douala canoe made from?
questions.explanation: Traditional Douala canoes are carved from mangrove wood, which is abundant in their coastal environment.

questions.question: Which Douala tradition involves elaborate masquerades?
questions.explanation: Douala masquerades are used in various ceremonies including weddings, funerals, and initiations.

questions.question: What is the name of the traditional Douala royal regalia?
questions.explanation: The Paramount Chief's Regalia includes traditional items of authority for Douala rulers.

questions.question: Which Douala custom involves the exchange of gifts during first meetings?
questions.explanation: The kola nut ceremony is a traditional Douala custom of exchanging kola nuts as a sign of hospitality.

questions.question: What is the traditional Douala art of decorating calabashes called?
questions.explanation: The Douala traditionally engrave calabashes with intricate patterns for ceremonial use.

questions.question: Which Douala tradition involves the use of traditional medicine?
questions.explanation: Nganga are traditional healers among the Douala who use herbs and spiritual practices for healing.

fulani-culture.name: Fulani Culture
fulani-culture.description: Discover the nomadic traditions and culture of the Fulani people in Cameroon.

questions.question: What is the primary economic activity of the Fulani people?
questions.explanation: The Fulani are traditionally nomadic pastoralists known for their cattle herding.

questions.question: Which animal is most sacred to the Fulani?
questions.explanation: Cattle are considered sacred to the Fulani and are central to their culture and economy.

questions.question: What is the traditional Fulani dwelling called?
questions.explanation: The Fulani traditionally live in portable tents made from woven reeds or cloth.

questions.question: Which Fulani tradition involves elaborate body art?
questions.explanation: Scarification is a traditional Fulani practice of creating decorative scars on the body.

questions.question: What is the traditional Fulani language called?
questions.explanation: Fulfulde is the traditional language of the Fulani people, spoken across West Africa.

questions.question: Which Fulani custom involves the elaborate decoration of cattle?
questions.explanation: The Fulani adorn their cattle with bells, beads, and paint as a sign of beauty and status.

questions.question: What is the traditional Fulani marriage ceremony called?
questions.explanation: The traditional Fulani marriage ceremony is called Nikkah, incorporating Islamic traditions.

questions.question: Which Fulani tradition involves seasonal migration?
questions.explanation: Transhumance is the seasonal migration of Fulani herders with their cattle to find grazing land.

questions.question: What is the traditional Fulani musical instrument?
questions.explanation: The Fulani traditionally play flutes in their musical performances.

questions.question: What is the Fulani initiation ceremony for young boys called?
questions.explanation: The Fulani practice a form of initiation called 'slitting' for young boys, involving a ceremonial cut.

beti-people.name: Beti People
beti-people.description: Learn about the Beti ethnic group and their contributions to Cameroonian culture.

questions.question: Which region is primarily inhabited by the Beti people?
questions.explanation: The Beti people primarily inhabit the Central Region of Cameroon, including the capital Yaoundé.

questions.question: What is the traditional Beti farming system called?
questions.explanation: The Beti traditionally practice shifting cultivation, moving their farms to new areas after several years.

questions.question: Which Beti sub-group is known for their royal traditions?
questions.explanation: The Ewondo sub-group of the Beti people are known for their royal traditions and were historically centered in Yaoundé.

questions.question: What is the traditional Beti initiation ceremony called?
questions.explanation: Nkwe is the traditional Beti initiation ceremony marking the transition from childhood to adulthood.

questions.question: Which traditional Beti musical instrument is made from a hollowed log?
questions.explanation: Ekwe is a traditional Beti musical instrument made from a hollowed log, used for communication and ceremonies.

questions.question: What is the traditional Beti house called?
questions.explanation: The traditional Beti house is called an Obu, typically made with mud walls and a thatched roof.

questions.question: Which Beti tradition involves ancestor worship?
questions.explanation: The Beti people traditionally practice ancestor worship as part of their spiritual beliefs.

questions.question: What is the Beti traditional masked dance called?
questions.explanation: Ekang is the traditional Beti masked dance performed during ceremonies and festivals.

questions.question: Which crop is most important in Beti agriculture?
questions.explanation: Cassava is the most important crop in Beti agriculture, serving as a staple food.

questions.question: What is the Beti traditional system of governance called?
questions.explanation: The Beti traditionally organize themselves into chiefdoms led by chiefs who govern local communities.

cameroon-economy.name: Cameroon Economy
cameroon-economy.description: Explore the economic sectors, resources, and development of Cameroon.

questions.question: Which sector contributes the most to Cameroon's GDP?
questions.explanation: The services sector contributes the most to Cameroon's GDP, followed by agriculture and industry.

questions.question: What is Cameroon's main agricultural export?
questions.explanation: Cocoa is Cameroon's main agricultural export, with the country being one of the world's largest cocoa producers.

questions.question: Which oil field is the largest in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: The Rio del Rey oil field is the largest oil field in Cameroon, located in the Southwest Region.

questions.question: What is the currency of Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Cameroon uses the Central African CFA franc (XAF) as its currency.

questions.question: Which port is Cameroon's main seaport?
questions.explanation: Douala Port is Cameroon's main seaport and the largest in Central Africa.

questions.question: What percentage of Cameroon's population is employed in agriculture?
questions.explanation: Approximately 70% of Cameroon's population is employed in the agricultural sector.

questions.question: Which mineral is Cameroon a significant producer of?
questions.explanation: Cameroon produces significant quantities of gold, bauxite, and oil.

questions.question: What is the main industrial sector in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Cameroon's main industrial sectors include textiles, food processing, and cement production.

questions.question: Which international organization provides significant aid to Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Cameroon receives significant aid from the World Bank, IMF, UNDP, and other international organizations.

questions.question: What is Cameroon's main trading partner?
questions.explanation: France is Cameroon's main trading partner due to historical ties from the colonial period.

cameroon-politics.name: Cameroon Politics
cameroon-politics.description: Understand the political system, governance, and leadership of Cameroon.

questions.question: What type of government does Cameroon have?
questions.explanation: Cameroon has a presidential republic system of government.

questions.question: Who is the current president of Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Paul Biya has been the president of Cameroon since 1982.

questions.question: How many terms can a president serve in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: There is no term limit for presidents in Cameroon.

questions.question: What is the name of the lower house of Cameroon's parliament?
questions.explanation: The National Assembly is the lower house of Cameroon's bicameral parliament.

questions.question: Which political party has been dominant in Cameroon since independence?
options: National Union for Democracy and Progress
questions.explanation: The Cameroon People's Democratic Movement (CPDM) has been the dominant political party since independence.

questions.question: What is the minimum voting age in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: The minimum voting age in Cameroon is 18 years old.

questions.question: Which constitution is currently in effect in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: The 1972 Constitution is currently in effect in Cameroon, though it has been amended several times.

questions.question: What is the official name of Cameroon in French?
questions.explanation: The official name of Cameroon in French is 'Cameroun'.

questions.question: Which international court has Cameroon been involved with regarding border disputes?
options: African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights
questions.explanation: Cameroon has been involved with the International Court of Justice regarding border disputes with Nigeria.

questions.question: What is the capital city of Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Yaoundé is the capital city of Cameroon.

cameroon-culture.name: Cameroon Culture & Heritage
cameroon-culture.description: Test your knowledge about the rich culture, traditions, and history of Cameroon.

questions.question: What are the two official languages of Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Cameroon has two official languages: French and English, reflecting its colonial history.

questions.question: Which of these is a traditional Cameroonian music genre?
questions.explanation: Makossa is a traditional Cameroonian music genre that originated in Douala.

questions.question: What is the traditional Cameroonian wrestling style called?
questions.explanation: Guelewar is the traditional Cameroonian wrestling style, particularly popular among the Sawa people.

questions.question: Which Cameroonian artist is known as the 'Grandfather of Makossa'?
questions.explanation: Manu Dibango is known as the 'Grandfather of Makossa' and helped popularize the genre internationally.

questions.question: What is the traditional Cameroonian dish made from cassava and eru called?
questions.explanation: Waterfufu and eru is a traditional Cameroonian dish made from cassava and a leafy vegetable called eru.

questions.question: Which ethnic group is known for the Ngondo festival?
questions.explanation: The Sawa people are known for the Ngondo festival, a water festival held at the coast.

questions.question: What is the traditional Cameroonian hut style called?
questions.explanation: Rondavel is the traditional Cameroonian hut style, characterized by its round shape and conical roof.

questions.question: Which Cameroonian city is known as the 'economic capital'?
questions.explanation: Douala is known as the 'economic capital' of Cameroon due to its port and commercial activities.

questions.question: What is the traditional Cameroonian masked dance called?
questions.explanation: Ekang is a traditional Cameroonian masked dance performed during ceremonies and festivals.

questions.question: Which lake in Cameroon is known for its floating villages?
questions.explanation: Lake Ossa in Cameroon is known for its floating villages inhabited by the Sawa people.

bamileke-heritage.name: Bamileke Heritage
bamileke-heritage.description: Explore the rich traditions and history of the Bamileke people.

questions.question: What is the traditional title of a Bamileke ruler?
questions.explanation: The traditional ruler of Bamileke chiefdoms is called a Fon.

questions.question: Which of these is a traditional Bamileke textile?
questions.explanation: Toghu is a traditional embroidered cloth worn by the Bamileke people during important ceremonies.

questions.question: What is the primary economic activity of the Bamileke people?
questions.explanation: The Bamileke are known for their agricultural skills and entrepreneurial spirit in trade.

questions.question: In which region of Cameroon do the Bamileke primarily live?
questions.explanation: The Bamileke people primarily inhabit the Western Highlands of Cameroon.

questions.question: What is the significance of the 'Lem' festival among the Bamileke?
questions.explanation: The Lem festival is an annual harvest celebration marking the new yam harvest.

questions.question: What is the name of the traditional Bamileke royal dance?
questions.explanation: Ngyem is the traditional royal dance of the Bamileke people, performed during ceremonies.

questions.question: Which Bamileke tradition involves elaborate bronze work?
questions.explanation: The Bamileke are renowned for their bronze casting, particularly royal regalia and ceremonial objects.

questions.question: What is the traditional Bamileke coming-of-age ceremony called?
questions.explanation: Nkwe is the traditional Bamileke initiation ceremony marking the transition to adulthood.

questions.question: Which crop is most associated with Bamileke agriculture?
questions.explanation: Yams are the most important crop in Bamileke agriculture and are central to their culture.

questions.question: What is the traditional Bamileke house called?
questions.explanation: Tchogol is the traditional Bamileke house, typically rectangular with a thatched roof.

cameroon-environment.name: Cameroon Environment
cameroon-environment.description: Learn about Cameroon's biodiversity, conservation efforts, and environmental challenges.

questions.question: Which national park in Cameroon is known for its mountain gorillas?
questions.explanation: Cross River National Park is known for its population of Cross River gorillas, a critically endangered subspecies.

questions.question: What is the main threat to Cameroon's rainforests?
questions.explanation: Cameroon's rainforests face threats from logging, mining, and agricultural expansion.

questions.question: Which endangered species is found in Cameroon's forests?
questions.explanation: Cameroon's forests are home to African elephants, western lowland gorillas, and chimpanzees, all of which are endangered.

questions.question: What is Cameroon's largest lake?
questions.explanation: Lake Chad is Cameroon's largest lake, though it's shared with neighboring countries.

questions.question: Which environmental disaster occurred at Lake Nyos in 1986?
questions.explanation: In 1986, a gas explosion at Lake Nyos released a deadly cloud of carbon dioxide, killing over 1,700 people.

questions.question: What percentage of Cameroon is covered by forests?
questions.explanation: Approximately 50% of Cameroon's land area is covered by forests.

questions.question: Which Cameroon mountain is known for its unique ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Mount Cameroon is known for its unique ecosystem with diverse flora and fauna due to its volcanic nature.

questions.question: What is the main conservation area for elephants in Cameroon?
questions.explanation: Bouba Ndjida National Park is the main conservation area for elephants in Cameroon.

questions.question: Which Cameroon region has the highest biodiversity?
questions.explanation: The Southern Region of Cameroon has the highest biodiversity due to its extensive rainforests and diverse ecosystems.

questions.question: What is Cameroon's main environmental challenge?
questions.explanation: Deforestation is Cameroon's main environmental challenge, threatening biodiversity and contributing to climate change.

baka-folklore.name: Baka Folklore
baka-folklore.description: Learn about the forest traditions and stories of the Baka people.

questions.question: What was the name of the Baka girl who learned to listen to the forest?
questions.explanation: Mbeya was the Baka girl who learned to communicate with the forest by listening closely.

questions.question: Which tree is considered sacred in the Baka legend?
questions.explanation: The sacred ceiba tree spoke to Mbeya and taught her how to listen to the forest.

questions.question: How do the Baka people know where to find food and water?
questions.explanation: The Baka listen to the leaves, birds, and insects to find their way in the forest.

questions.question: What is the Baka people's relationship with the forest?
options: The forest is their mother, father, and friend
They see the forest as an enemy
questions.correct: The forest is their mother, father, and friend
questions.explanation: The Baka have a deep spiritual and practical connection with the forest, which provides for all their needs.

questions.question: What happened when Mbeya listened to the forest?
questions.explanation: By listening to the forest's voice, Mbeya was able to find her way back to her village.

kapsiki-folklore.name: Kapsiki Folklore
kapsiki-folklore.description: Discover the mountain legends and traditions of the Kapsiki people.

questions.question: What is the name of the Kapsiki sacred mountain?
questions.explanation: Rhumsiki is the sacred mountain of the Kapsiki people, where the mountain spirits reside.

questions.question: What natural disaster struck the Kapsiki people long ago?
questions.explanation: A terrible drought dried up the rivers and crops, leaving the Kapsiki hungry and thirsty.

questions.question: What did the chief do at dawn on the sacred mountain?
options: Struck a big black rock with his staff
Climbed to the top and sang
Left a sacrifice for the spirits
questions.correct: Struck a big black rock with his staff
questions.explanation: Following the spirit's instructions, the chief struck the sacred rock at dawn, and water began to flow.

questions.question: What kind of homes do the Kapsiki people build?
options: Homes on terraces up the mountain
questions.correct: Homes on terraces up the mountain
questions.explanation: The Kapsiki build their homes on terraces that climb up the steep Mandara Mountains.

questions.question: What do the Kapsiki people do to honor the mountain spirits?
options: Make offerings on the sacred rock
questions.correct: Make offerings on the sacred rock
questions.explanation: The Kapsiki make annual offerings on the sacred rock at Rhumsiki to thank the mountain spirits.

massa-folklore.name: Massa Folklore
massa-folklore.description: Explore the cattle traditions and stories of the Massa people.

questions.question: What animal is most important to the Massa people?
questions.explanation: Cattle are central to Massa culture, economy, and traditions.

questions.question: Where did the Massa's first cattle come from?
options: From Lake Chad, led by a spirit bull
questions.correct: From Lake Chad, led by a spirit bull
questions.explanation: According to legend, the first cattle came out of Lake Chad, led by a spirit in the shape of a giant bull.

questions.question: Who was the young Massa man who received the cattle?
questions.explanation: Bako was the young Massa man who met the spirit bull and received the first cattle.

questions.question: What do the Massa use to decorate their cattle during festivals?
questions.explanation: During festivals, Massa men decorate their cattle with colorful cloth and beads to show their beauty and status.

questions.question: What do the Massa believe about the spirit bull?
options: The spirit bull still watches over them and their herds
The spirit bull lives in the forest
The spirit bull returns every full moon
The spirit bull is a god
questions.correct: The spirit bull still watches over them and their herds
questions.explanation: The Massa believe the spirit of the giant bull still watches over them and their herds to this day.

yamba-folklore.name: Yamba Folklore
yamba-folklore.description: Learn about the peace traditions and sacred pipe of the Yamba people.

questions.question: What is the Yamba people's most sacred object?
questions.explanation: The Sacred Pipe of Peace is the most sacred object of the Yamba people, used to make peace agreements.

questions.question: Who gave the sacred pipe to the Yamba chief?
questions.explanation: The chief's ancestor appeared to him in a dream and gave him the Sacred Pipe of Peace.

questions.question: What happened when the two chiefs smoked the pipe together?
options: They forgot their anger and hatred and made peace
questions.correct: They forgot their anger and hatred and made peace
questions.explanation: Smoking the pipe together made the two chiefs forget their differences and establish a lasting peace.

questions.question: What is the Yamba people's reputation?
questions.explanation: The Yamba people are known for their peace-loving nature and their respect for elders and ancestors.

questions.question: Where is the Sacred Pipe of Peace kept today?
questions.explanation: The Sacred Pipe of Peace is kept in the chief's palace and used to resolve disputes.

chamba-folklore.name: Chamba Folklore
chamba-folklore.description: Discover the drum traditions and ancestor stories of the Chamba people.

questions.question: What is the most sacred musical instrument of the Chamba people?
questions.explanation: The Drum of the Ancestors is the Chamba's most sacred instrument, able to call the spirits for help.

questions.question: What did the ancestors give the Chamba chief?
questions.explanation: The ancestors appeared to the chief in a dream and gave him the Drum of the Ancestors.

questions.question: What happened when the chief played the drum?
options: The ancestors came out to fight alongside the Chamba
questions.correct: The ancestors came out to fight alongside the Chamba
questions.explanation: When the chief played the Drum of the Ancestors, the ancestors emerged from the hills to help the Chamba defeat their enemies.

questions.question: Where do the Chamba people live?
questions.explanation: The Chamba people live in the Adamawa Region of Cameroon, known for its rolling hills and plains.

questions.question: What do the Chamba drum music tell?
options: Stories of their history, victories, and love for their land
questions.correct: Stories of their history, victories, and love for their land
questions.explanation: Chamba drum music tells stories of their history, victories, and their deep love for their land and people.

mousgoum-folklore.name: Mousgoum Folklore
mousgoum-folklore.description: Explore the unique beehive hut traditions of the Mousgoum people.

questions.question: What do the Mousgoum call their traditional homes?
options: The Huts of a Thousand Smiles
questions.correct: The Huts of a Thousand Smiles
questions.explanation: The Mousgoum call their traditional homes 'The Huts of a Thousand Smiles' because they bring happiness and comfort.

questions.question: What shape are the Mousgoum's traditional huts?
questions.explanation: The Mousgoum's traditional huts are round and have a high, curved roof, like a beehive.

questions.question: Who learned to build the huts from a spirit?
questions.explanation: Zara was the Mousgoum woman who learned to build the beehive huts from a spirit in a dream.

questions.question: What is special about the Mousgoum huts?
options: They are cool in summer and warm in winter
questions.correct: They are cool in summer and warm in winter
questions.explanation: The Mousgoum's beehive huts are naturally cool in the summer and warm in the winter.

questions.question: Where do the Mousgoum people live?
options: Far North Region, near the Logone River
questions.correct: Far North Region, near the Logone River
questions.explanation: The Mousgoum people live in the Far North Region of Cameroon, near the Logone River.

toupouri-folklore.name: Toupouri Folklore
toupouri-folklore.description: Learn about the river traditions and fish festival of the Toupouri people.

questions.question: Which river is central to Toupouri life?
questions.explanation: The Benue River is the center of life for the Toupouri people, providing water and fish.

questions.question: What is the Toupouri's most important festival?
questions.explanation: The Festival of the Fish is the most important Toupouri festival, honoring the river spirits.

questions.question: Who appeared to the Toupouri chief in a dream?
options: The spirit of the Benue River
questions.correct: The spirit of the Benue River
questions.explanation: A beautiful woman, the spirit of the Benue River, appeared to the chief in a dream.

questions.question: What happens during the fishing competition at the festival?
options: The person who catches the biggest fish is honored as a hero
Everyone fishes at the same time
Fish are released back into the river
questions.correct: The person who catches the biggest fish is honored as a hero
questions.explanation: During the Festival of the Fish, there is a big fishing competition, and the person who catches the biggest fish is honored as a hero.

questions.question: What do the Toupouri do to thank the river spirits?
options: Hold the Festival of the Fish
questions.correct: Hold the Festival of the Fish
questions.explanation: The Toupouri hold the Festival of the Fish every year to thank the river spirits for their gifts of water and fish.

bafia-folklore.name: Bafia Folklore
bafia-folklore.description: Explore the elephant king and nature traditions of the Bafia people.

questions.question: Who is the protector of the Bafia people?
questions.explanation: The King of the Elephants is the protector of the Bafia people and their land.

questions.question: What did the hunters want to do?
options: Kill the King of the Elephants for his ivory tusks
Capture the King of the Elephants for a zoo
Follow the King of the Elephants to find treasure
Make friends with the King of the Elephants
questions.correct: Kill the King of the Elephants for his ivory tusks
questions.explanation: Some greedy hunters wanted to kill the King of the Elephants to get his precious ivory tusks.

questions.question: What did the King of the Elephants do when the hunters came?
options: He made a great rain fall that washed away their tracks and made them lost
He called the other elephants for help
questions.correct: He made a great rain fall that washed away their tracks and made them lost
questions.explanation: The King of the Elephants made a great rain fall that washed away the hunters' tracks and made them lost in the forest.

questions.question: What do the Bafia people believe about respecting nature?
options: If they respect nature, it will take care of them in return
Nature is an enemy to be conquered
Nature must be exploited for profit
questions.correct: If they respect nature, it will take care of them in return
questions.explanation: The Bafia believe that if they respect nature, the forest and animals will take care of them in return.

questions.question: Where do the Bafia people live?
questions.explanation: The Bafia people live in the Central Region of Cameroon, in an area of lush forests and fertile farmland.
